claim: Consolidating all agent tools into a single MCP server eliminates duplicated tool-call failure evaluations across multiple agent teams and reduces the overall evaluation burden
actionable: Centralize your agent tools into a single MCP server rather than building separate tools for each agent team, which consolidates evals and maintenance into one place
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Yak: Building MCP Servers That Make Agents More Effective

claim: Agent-agnostic evals for MCP servers should optimize for final results rather than specific tool calls or agent behavior, enabling any agent to use the server well
actionable: When writing evals for MCP servers, test for correct final outcomes rather than forcing specific tool sequences, allowing agents creative freedom in problem-solving
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Yak: Building MCP Servers That Make Agents More Effective

claim: Tool-agnostic evals prevent brittle failure modes where agents need to be forced to use specific tools, and instead allow agents to discover multiple valid paths to the same outcome
actionable: Avoid writing evals that mandate specific tool calls; instead test whether the agent achieved the goal, even if it used a different combination of tools than expected
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Yak: Building MCP Servers That Make Agents More Effective

claim: MCP server evals can be generated automatically by converting SQL queries to natural language questions, eliminating the need to manually write hundreds of eval scenarios
actionable: Use LLMs to generate natural language questions from your API's SQL queries and parse these into eval scenarios, then seed these with actual data to create labeled eval pairs
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Yak: Building MCP Servers That Make Agents More Effective

claim: Ambiguous eval scenarios (e.g., 'recent logs' or 'last hour') fail when run at different times; fixing temporal references (using time travel or fixed timestamps) makes evals deterministic and repeatable
actionable: When creating MCP server evals, remove temporal ambiguity by fixing timestamps or using a time-travel mechanism so evals produce consistent results regardless of when they're run
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Yak: Building MCP Servers That Make Agents More Effective

claim: Datadog's MCP server eval runs complete in 2 minutes and integrate with observability dashboards, enabling a dev workflow where changes are tested and visualized instantly
actionable: Instrument your MCP server evals with observability (trace IDs, LLM calls, tool calls) so developers can see failures at a glance and share debugging traces with their team
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Yak: Building MCP Servers That Make Agents More Effective

claim: Tool descriptions and tool response formatting are critical: agents see both the description (to decide which tool to call) and the response (to decide what to do next), making both parts of the search space for optimization
actionable: Optimize both tool descriptions and tool response formatting (not just the API call), as agents use both to decide what to do next in their loop
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Yak: Building MCP Servers That Make Agents More Effective
